subject: Joint Distributions
topic: §45 Joint Probability Distributions of Functions of Random Variables
question: Let $X_1$ and $X_2$ be two continuous random variables with joint density function $f_{X_1 X_2}(x_1, x_2) = \begin{cases} e^{-(x_1 + x_2)} & x_1 \ge 0, x_2 \ge 0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ Let $Y_1 = X_1 + X_2$ and $Y_2 = \frac{X_1}{X_1 + X_2}$. Find the joint density function of $Y_1$ and $Y_2$.
answer: $f_{Y_1 Y_2}(y_1, y_2) = \begin{cases} e^{-y_1} y_1 & y_1 \ge 0, 0 < y_2 < 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$
explanation: Answer: $f_{Y_1 Y_2}(y_1, y_2) = \begin{cases} e^{-y_1} y_1 & y_1 \ge 0, 0 < y_2 < 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$
